preview_text:
The model backs Turkey to win at 49%, while Paraguay sits at 21%—a gap suggesting a clear but not overwhelming favorite. Turkey's win probability outpaces the underdog's by roughly 27 percentage points, placing this match in the moderately lopsided range rather than among the tightest contests.

The draw probability of 30% indicates genuine competitive balance despite Turkey's edge in outright win chances. This suggests a match in which Paraguay can realistically force a stalemate or exploit moments of vulnerability, even if the model expects Turkey to convert more of its attacking opportunities into decisive outcomes over 90 minutes.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge meaningfully on the favorite's chances. Books assign Turkey only 41%, some 7 percentage points below the model's 49% assessment. Both sources agree the draw is plausible at roughly 30%, but books lean substantially toward Paraguay at 28% versus the model's 21%. The dashboard does not claim to know which reading is correct; the gap warrants monitoring as match conditions become clearer.
